text: Create a file named domain_list.txt at the /etc/mosmb/ location and mention the domain to which the Mo SMB server is joined.
Command : cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt Output : mosmbad.dom mosmbad2.dom

Create a file named /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt In new releases, use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries should be in the format of spn@DOMAIN , similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive. Entries in /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt, must match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Command : cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt Output : cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad2.dom@MOSMBAD2.DOM
In order for Mo SMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.

To generate a ticket on the server run command kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . The command asks password for the specified user. Make sure ticket is taken for all the domains to which Mo SMB server is getting configured for.

Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser1 (domain user of mosmbad.dom), mosmbad2.dom, mouser2 (domain user of mosmbad2.dom) Command : sudo kinit -V mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Output : Password for mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM: Command : sudo kinit -V mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM Output : Password for mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For Mo SMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab

Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser1 (domain user of mosmbad.dom), mosmbad2.dom, mouser2 (domain user of mosmbad2.dom) [all domain users entries present mosmb.keytab] Command : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Command : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM

1. The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials. 2. Check the ticket time with klist -A, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, Mo SMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on Mo SMB service start.

Command : sudo klist -A Output : Ticket cache: DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt/tktAD124 Default principal: mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting Expires Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50 01/16/2020 21:33:50 krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 01/16/2020 11:33:50 01/16/2020 21:33:50 ldap/<AD1 name>.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 Ticket cache: DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt/tkt12qun Default principal: mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM Valid starting Expires Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50 01/16/2020 21:33:50 krbtgt/MOSMBAD2.DOM@MOSMBAD2.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 01/16/2020 11:33:50 01/16/2020 21:33:50 ldap/<AD2 name>.mosmbad2.dom@MOSMBAD2.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management   Kerberos Policy   Security Settings in the AD. 3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart. 4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at MoSMB start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

Mo SMB has certain dependencies that you need to install on the server machine/s. Dependencies will be promoted to the user while installing the Mo SMB package on the server machine/s on which Mo SMB is not installed previously. Note : Install only the pre-dependencies that are prompted. The OS dependent package versions change according to the server OS distribution.
Note: for centOS jemalloc is available in EPEL repository, EPEL is "Extra Packages for Enterprise Linux", so yum install epel-release -y yum install jemalloc -y

heimdal-libs or heimdal packages for support libraries of Heimdal Kerberos Note : Depending on the Mo SMB Edition provided following packages will also be needed. The OS dependent package versions change according to the server OS distribution. Note : For kerberos and ntlmad (ntlmssp) authentication on Mo SMB following packages will also be needed.

The OS dependent package versions may change according to the server OS distribution realm or adcli or Power Broker Identity Services (PBIS) any domain join utility. adcli package is required for ntlmad (ntlmssp) authentication specifically. Note : Also cron and logrotate related packages are needed according to the server OS distribution.

User will be provided XPKG-Mo SMB script<XPKG-Mo SMB-....sh> according to the OS distribution. In this script, the Mo SMB package, Ansible tar and  Mo SMB License Agreement  is embedded. One can run this script to extract Mo SMB package and Ansible tar after accepting  Mo SMB License Agreement . For the successful execution of this script, install the ncurses library package When user runs the script, Mo SMB License Agreement page pops up.
User can either Decline or Accept and Extract the EULA, and once accepted, the EULA will be saved at /opt/mosmb directory along with the package.

Provided XPKG-MoSMB script : XPKG-MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20220303_0.centos-8.x86_64.sh Command : ./XPKG-MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20220303_0.centos-8.x86_64.sh Output : MoSMB EULA is present at : /opt/mosmb/MoSMB_EULA.pdf MoSMB ansible is present at : /opt/mosmb/ MoSMB package is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install MoSMB zookeeper rpm is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install

Depending on OS distribution and Mo SMB version, user will get Mo SMB package at location  /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install  on accepting Mo SMB License Agreement. Cent OS distribution users will receive the .rpm package of Mo SMB. And for Cluster builds, a zookeeper rpm will also be provided along with Mo SMB rpm, which can be used for zookeeper installation. Ubuntu and Debian distribution users will receive the .deb package of Mo SMB.

Naming convention used for Mo SMB packages - Standalone Builds : Mo SMB-SA Cluster Builds : Mo SMB-CL S3 Builds : Mo SMB-SA-S3 The /etc/mosmb and /opt/mosmb directories will be created on successful installation (if not present already). Configuration files for Mo SMB mosmb.json and mosmb_logging.conf will be copied to the /etc/mosmb/ location. s3.json will be copied at the /etc/mosmb/ location depending on the Mo SMB version.

Plugin files will be located at /usr/local/lib/mosmb/ depending on the build type libios_vfs.so libmosmb_pst_plugin.so and libios_s3.so will be part of installation. For hourly basis log rotation mologrotate will be kept under /etc/cron.hourly/ /etc/mosmb/scripts has multiple scripts including Performance tuning, zookeeper cleanup etc
Install the .rpm package extracted at location /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install with the following command:

Provided rpm package name : MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64.rpm Command : cd /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install Command : rpm -ivh MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64.rpm Output : Preparing... ################################# [100%] Updating / installing... 1:MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0 ################################# [100%] Running performance tuning script..Done. MoSMB is now installed. Please configure licensing and shares before first run. Run MoSMB as service with "service mosmb start"

Un-installing Mo SMB will remove all the files created by Mo SMB during installation from the server machine except the Mo SMB log files namely /var/log/mosmb.log and /var/log/mosmb_audit.log and other user created Mo SMB files and directories. Also mosmb.keytab, mosmb_u.dat files are left behind in /etc/mosmb/. So next time u reinstall Mo SMB package the mosmb users related data and any keytab if generated is available for the mosmb service to use.

Command : rpm -qa | grep MoSMB Output : MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64 Command : rpm -e MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64 OR rpm -e MoSMB-SA Output : MoSMB service is not running (The output may differ according to the MoSMB status.)

You can upgrade Mo SMB directly instead of uninstalling Mo SMB and then installing the new Mo SMB package. This activity has the following dependencies: 1. The Mo SMB version of the new package should be higher than the installed version. (A Mo SMB-3.1.1 package cannot upgrade to Mo SMB-3.1.0) 2. The mosmb.json config version in the new package should be higher or same (we support upgrade from config version 1.4.0 to config versions above those. e.g. to 1.5.0 or 1.6.0 etc) 3.

The Mo SMB version could be same but the package names must be different. (A Mo SMB-3.1.1_0 user can upgrade to Mo SMB-3.1.1_1) 4. Upgrade should be from same type of Mo SMB package (i.e. One can upgrade a Mo SMB-SA version to a Mo SMB-SA version only and not to a Mo SMB-CL version) Note : An Mo SMB upgrade will keep the previous Mo SMB configuration file with name mosmb.json.orig at the /etc/mosmb/ location and install the latest mosmb.json version while preserving your old config values.
The etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file from previous version will be preserved.

You - the user can name a share as per your requirement. The default share names are share1 and share2 . The maximum length of a share name can be 80 characters. The share name can contain spaces, underscores( _ ) and alphanumeric strings.

This is a comment added for the share. User can add a message if required or it can be kept blank. This parameter is for the user's reference only. Message added here is displayed as a tool tip on hovering the share when accessed from client side.
Share path is the path of the share that the user wishes to export as an Mo SMB share. State the complete path of the share to be exported.
The is Continously Available field is set to yes by default. The user requires a continuous availability of the exported share at the client end.

The user should set the is Cluster to no and enable High Availability global Section parameter to no when server is in Standalone Mode The user should set the is Cluster to yes and node Id starts from 1 . For more configuration settings in the cluster mode refer to the Single Domain Cluster Mode / Multi Domain Cluster Mode . For the clustering to work the user must set the enable High Availability global Section parameter to yes in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.conf or /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.

mosmb.json representation - Mo SMB Cluster settings for share mosmb.json representation - Mo SMB Cluster settings for share mosmb.json representation - Mo SMB Cluster settings for share mosmb.json representation - Mo SMB Cluster settings for share mosmb.conf representation - Mo SMB Cluster settings for share mosmb.conf representation - Mo SMB Cluster settings for share

Encryption does not support on-disk data encryption it is encryption over the wire. Encryption will encrypt the network layer packet exchanges. Set the share Encrypt value in the share level settings to yes if desired. For Share encryption to work the user must set the Global level parameter share Level Encryption to yes in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.conf or "/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json" file.

When the reject Unencrypted Access is set to yes in the Global level parameter, un-encrypted requests from the client side are not served. The user can set this parameter to yes or no as per the requirement. Note : When global Encrypt Data from the global level parameters is set to yes , all the packets are encrypted for the server client communication. This is irrespective of the share level encryption settings.
If share is mounted on a case sensitive disk, Set this to 'yes' If share is mounted on a case insensitive disk, Set this to 'no'

Mo SMB support MS-Dfs links. MS-Dfs links support. If there are MS-Dfs links in the Mo SMB share then keep this parameter's value to yes, otherwise no. When ms Dfs Support is set to yes metadata Store should be set to xattr. Mo SMB -S3 edition does not support ms-dfs currently, so it is recommended for Mo SMB-S3 edition to set ms Dfs Support to no. To Create and user dfs link with mosmb

File system information to be used (fs Info Auto Detect, sectors Per Unit, bytes Per Sector) Specify the file system information to be used for this share. If "fs Info Auto Detect" is "no" then values of the "sectors Per Unit" and "bytes Per Sector" will be taken from the configuration file i.e. "mosmb.json" If "fs Info Auto Detect" is "yes" then values of the "sectors Per Unit" and "bytes Per Sector" will be taken by using the systme call.
So in that case, the values of "sectors Per Unit" and "bytes Per Sector" mentioned in the configuration file i.e. "mosmb.json" will get ignored.
Example : "fsInfo": { "fsInfoAutoDetect": "yes", "sectorsPerUnit": 1, "bytesPerSector": 4096 }

An Access Control List (ACL) helps the user with share access authorization. Mo SMB provides three types of ACL strategies currently, none, posix, advance Posix and ntacl Mo SMB -S3 edition does not support ACL's currently, so it is recommended for Mo SMB-S3 edition to set ACL to none.
Setting ACL to none means there will be no authorization required for creating, modifying or deleting data from the share with the ACL strategy set to none .
When "acl": "posix", is used Mo SMB permits the underlying filesystem to take a decision for the shared file/directory's read and write permissions.
"aclStrategy":{ "acl": "posix", "checkPosixPermissionBit": "yes" },

is used Mo SMB takes the decision for the shared file/directory's read and write permissions. In case of posix ACL, Mo SMB maps the Active Directory user who has established a SMB session to an equivalent Linux user. The effective User ID (UID) of this user is used by the file system to check access. To facilitate this, Mo SMB needs to populate nsswitch.conf to use a proper ID mapper.
Example:  SSSD In case of the posix ACL and Kerberos authentication the user is required to set the permissions of the share path to a domain user and domain group by running the chown command.
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user), /opt/share Command : sudo chown -R ''mouser@mosmbad.dom'':''domain users@mosmbad.dom'' /opt/share

In case of posix ACL, Mo SMB maps the Active Directory user who has established a SMB session to an equivalent Linux user. The effective User ID (UID) of this user is used by the file system to check access. To facilitate this, Mo SMB needs to populate the nsswitch.conf file to use a proper ID mapper. Example : SSSD In case of the posix ACL and Kerberos authentication the user is required to set the permissions of the share path to a domain user and domain group by running the chown command.
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user), /opt/share Command : sudo chown -R ''MOSMBAD\mouser'':''MOSMBAD\domain users'' /opt/share

In case of the posix ACL and ntlm_local authentication the user is required: To set the permissions of the share path to a Linux local user. To add the user group as an Mo SMB user using the mosmb_passwd binary. Refer to the ntlm Local authentication mode settings for details.
In the case of advance Posix , authorization is done based on the advance permissions set from the server or client side. To set advance acl permissions use setfacl command.

Test Data : motest, mogroup, /opt/share Command1 : sudo setfacl -d -m -u:"motest@mosmbad.dom":rwx /opt/share (default permissions or inherited permissions for user) Command2 : sudo setfacl -m -u:"motest@mosmbad.dom":rwx /opt/share (user permissions set on the share path) Command3 : sudo setfacl -d -m -g:"mogroup@mosmbad.dom":r-x /opt/share (default permissions or inherited permissions for group) Command4 : sudo setfacl -m -u:"mogroup@mosmbad.dom":r-x /opt/share (group permissions set on the share path)

In the case of ntacl , authorization is done according to the Active Directory Users (similar to ntfs). ntacl rules are used to check access. In this case, Mo SMB uses the extended attributes provided by the file system for storing the ACLs. ntacl is not supported on "auth Mode": "ntlm Local" Warning: The Mo SMB Server will not start successfully when the "auth Mode": "ntlm Local" and "acl": "ntacl", . Note : The ntacl authorization mode is not supported on the ntlm Local authentication mode.

User Based Enumeration (UBE) provides the configuration capability to control share visibility. It makes the share visible only to those users and groups that are listed in the configuration file. To enable and apply setting related to UBE per share, make necessary changes in ube Strategy section of shares Section Supported in mosmb.json version 1.2.0 onwards.
The users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group or secondary group can see the share. Nomenclature :
ubeUsers  : [ mo_user1@MOSMB_AD.DOM ],  ubeGroups  : [ domain users@MOSMB_AD.DOM ]
mosmb.json representation - ube Strategy for "ntlm Local" mosmb.json representation - ube Strategy for "ntlm Local"
The users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group or secondary group can see the share. Nomenclature :
ubeUsers  : [ mo_user1@MOSMB_AD ],  ubeGroups  : [ domain users@MOSMB_AD ]
Only the users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group can see the share. Nomenclature for domain joined clients :
ubeUsers  : [ user1 , user2@MOSMB_AD ],  ubeGroups  : [ group1 , group2@MOSMB_AD ]
ubeUsers : <mosmb_username> or <mosmb_username>@<client_hostname> ubeGroups : <group_name> or <group_name>@<client_hostname>
ubeUsers  : [ user1 , user2@my_client1 ],  ubeGroups  : [ group1 , group2@my_client1]

Specific permissions can be applied to newly created files or directories from the client end. This is an "octal value" that represents permissions. The default value for file permissions is set to 0644 [-rw-r--r--]. The default value for the directories permissions is set to 0755 [drwxr-xr-x].

Specify the storage strategy for the metadata storage. The values can be xattr or none . When the metadata Store is set to "none" it means that the Mo SMB server is not going to store any metadata associated with the file in the extended attribute space. When the metadata Store is set to "xattr" it means that the Mo SMB server will store the metadata associated with files or directories in the extended attribute space.

In this case, file metadata includes the security descriptor and smb file-attributes. When the authorization mode is "ntacl" the metadata storage must be "xattr". For Mo SMB -S3 edition the metadata storage must be "xattr"
Specifies whether to use O_DIRECT flag, in cases application uses FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING for operations. The deafult value is set to no .
The user is required to set the IOS plugin path. The default plugin path configuration is for the Virtual File System (VFS).

If mosmb service fails to start, check the mosmb.log from the log folder (Default is /var/log) for Error messages. In case there is json syntax error you can use the mosmb json syntax validation script

The mosmb.log should indicate if any configuration errors pertaining to the json parameters are encountered. Accordingly use following to correct thoes. Verify if the Mo SMB share path mentioned in the configuration actually exists on the server. Verify if the persistent store path mentioned in the Mo SMB configuration actually exists on the server. Verify if a valid combination of Authentication Modes and Authorization Modes is in use: i.

When the authentication mode is krb5 , the permitted authorization modes are posix and ntacl . ii. When the authentication is ntlm Local , the permitted authorization mode is posix . Verify if the plugin path mentioned in the Mo SMB configuration is correct. Verify that the performance parameters like number of threads, buffer size and pre-processor threads are not exceeding the available resources.
Refer to Performance Tuning Verify if the bind interface value mentioned is actually an active interface on the server. Refer to the Mo SMB configuration parameters for further details.

To start the Mo SMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the Mo SMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable. For more detailed information on Licensing refer to Mo SMB License Manager page

Verify if the Mo SMB server machine has a working internet connection. Verify if the Mo SMB server's network adapters are up. Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the Mo SMB license servers. Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the Mo SMB server. Verify if the time and date settings of the Mo SMB server are correct or up-to-date. Verify if a valid product key is provided in the Mo SMB configuration file for Licenses with product key.

Verify if the product key has reached the maximum number of activations limit. The information on the maximum limit for the number of machines the product key can be activated is given while providing the product key.

Verify if the Mo SMB server has a working internet connection. Verify if Mo SMB server's network adapters are up. Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the Mo SMB license servers. Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the Mo SMB server. Verify if the time and date settings for the Mo SMB server machine are correct or up-to-date.

Verify if the Mo SMB server's network adapters are up. Verify if the product key mentioned in the Mo SMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same. Make sure you have followed the steps for activation as per the offline product activation using product key

Verify if the trialresponse.xml file that is provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location. Verify if the trialrequest.xml is generated on the same Mo SMB server machine on which you need to activate the Mo SMB license. Verify if the Mo SMB server's network adapters are up.

When the Error - Failed to check product activation within grace period is seen in mosmb.log It would indicate that the product key was activated online on the server machine BUT now the server machine is not able to reach to | limelm site To activate the product key again, deactivate the current product key activation by running /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate Then follow offline activation steps
Check the mosmb.log for any obvious error messages that would indicate what is the problem. Check if mosmb service is running, check pid or process information using ps
# service mosmb status # pidof mosmb # ps -aux| grep mosmb

Verify if port 445 is open for the TCP communication. Verify if the TCP port mentioned in the Mo SMB configuration file is also configured on the client machine and is open for communication over TCP.

If error is related to krb5, Refer to the Troubleshooting for the AD Kerberos Mo SMB share access when authentication mode used for share access is krb5 If error is related to gss-api refer to the troubleshooting section for gss-api errors If the authentication mode is ntlm Local make sure that the (local linux) Mo SMB user is created on the Mo SMB server machine and you are accessing the share via the same user.

check the mosmb_audit.log for login from the user for whom the access is failing, to check if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in. Refer here for more details on the mosmb_audit.log If Login is successful, Verify if the user has the permission to access the share for the authentication mode used (krb5, ntlm-AD or ntlm_local) as mentioned in Permission Issues section below.

If a previously working node is removed and Re Joined to AD , for ntlm AD to work after such a change, you will need to re run mosmb_passwd -m and restart mosmb service too. Also old kerberos ticket on client side may prevent you from accessing the share. You need to purge the cache to remove old cluster/node kerberos ticket with klist purge OR user can sign out and signing to the client. OR The kerberos cache will expire in scheduled 10 hours and next access will renew the ticket automatically.
Verify if openssl configuration required for Ubuntu 22.04 are done If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required. Check openssl version with
[provider_sect] default = default_sect [default_sect] # activate = 1
[provider_sect] default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init] providers = provider_sect [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1

If access fails for a user, make sure audit logs are enabled and verify login attempts from the user for mosmb nodes. Check the mosmb_audit.log to see if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.

If login is successful check permission issues. If login is failed check the authentication password/domain information If no login attempt message is seen check mosmb.log for MO_ERROR or MO_WARN logs
Verify that the correct user permissions are set on the Mo SMB server side and the same user is used to access the share and create or edit the shared files or directories.

Verify if port 88 is open on both the Mo SMB server and the AD machine. This is required by the LDAP server. When the authentication mode is krb5 verify if the Kerberos ticket is present on the Mo SMB server. In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the LDAP client configuration settings are carried out. In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the SSSD related settings are carried out.
Verify if the package necessary for generating UUID is installed on the Mo SMB server. If not please install the package.

Verify if the sssd service is running on the Mo SMB server. check with id command that the AD user's details are retrieved and it does print the user ID and group IDs. In case it is failing to do so, try restarting the sssd service. Verify the nsswitch.conf file and make sure that .sss is mentioned in the configuration. Changes to the AD group membership are slow to take effect. The issue could be because of the sssd cache.

When you change the primary group of the user then the old entry in the sssd cache becomes invalid. For new changes to take effect restart the sssd service and run the following command: sss_cache -E OR sss_cache -u <user-name> If you are considering EVERYONE present in NTFS (Everyone includes the owner, group and other members) as Others (in Linux this means all the members other than the owner and group members) mapping will fail.
Make sure the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o. any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab.

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName

The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set:

Go to Active Directory users and computers User properties -> user account -> password never expires User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled, e.g. This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption You can also check the encryptions used in the keytab with -e option of klist klist -kte /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab

In mosmb.json, check that global Section is set to have enable High Availability to yes Check the o/p of klist -A to check a valid krb5 tkt is fetched during mosmb startup ( mosmb will use the mosmb.keytab and domain_list.txt to do a kinit ) Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies

If setup was working fine previously, and now we are getting "Preauthentication failed" error in mosmb.log, verify that the password for special user used for cluster keytab creation is not expired or changed. If so, need to recreate the mosmb.keytab using ktpass
Example : server.1 = <ip_server1>:4888:5888 server.2 = <ip_server2>:4888:5888 server.3 = <ip_server3>:4888:5888

Here 4888 used for follower nodes to connect to the leader node and 5888 used for leader election. 3. Mo SMB service not starting due to older zookeeper versions refer :- Uninstalling previous zookeeper version 4. Make sure after successful installation all required zookeeper libraries are present on the zookeeper server.

Go to the command line, become root and fire up your preferred editor (vi) by typing vi /etc/sysctl.conf Put the following into the newly created file,

chmod 0644 /etc/sysctl.conf from the command line
In order for Mo SMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.

1 Generating Kerberos Ticket 1.1 Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022) 1.2 Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022) 1.1 Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022) 1.2 Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, Mo SMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on Mo SMB service start.
To generate a ticket on the server run command kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . The command asks password for the specified user.
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command : sudo kinit -V mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Output : Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For Mo SMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user entry present mosmb.keytab) Command : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM

1. The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials. 2. Check the ticket time with klist -a, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)

Command : sudo klist -A Output : Ticket cache: FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt Default principal: mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting Expires Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50 01/16/2020 21:33:50 krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management   Kerberos Policy   Security Settings in the AD. 3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart. 4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at MoSMB start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

User can perform a Start, Stop or Restart operations on the Mo SMB service. For any change/s in the Mo SMB server configuration files /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json and/or /etc/mosmb/s3.json in any section/s it is mandatory to restart the Mo SMB server. Notes: : 1. systemctl support is available after release builds 073. 2. One can use Either service commands or systemctl commands throughout the cycle.
Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output : Starting MoSMB service...

When Mo SMB is being used with Zoo Keeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as Mo SMB is Zoo Keeper's client and Zoo Keeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server is exited abruptly or Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper - Command : sh /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Standalone Mode: Leader / Follower / Standalone this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup - ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress. To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output : Starting MoSMB service...

When Mo SMB is being used with Zoo Keeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as Mo SMB is Zoo Keeper client and Zoo Keeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server is exited abruptly or Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper on all nodes - Command : sh /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader Mode: Leader / Follower this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup on all nodes - ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress. To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output : Starting MoSMB service...
* If MoSMB server and ZooKeeper server are configured on 2 separate machines and only MoSMB service/server goes down then ZooKeeper cleanup is not a requirement.
Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output : Stopping MoSMB service...

When Mo SMB is being used with Zoo Keeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as Mo SMB is Zoo Keeper client and Zoo Keeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server is exited abruptly or Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service/s Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED

When Mo SMB is being used with Zoo Keeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as Mo SMB is Zoo Keeper client and Zoo Keeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server is exited abruptly or Mo SMB / Zoo Keeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service on all nodes - Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED
Command : EITHER service mosmb restart OR systemctl restart mosmb Output : Stopping MoSMB service... Starting MoSMB service...

In case Mo SMB goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing Mo SMB and/or zookeeper node/s to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the Mo SMB along with Mo SMB-Zoo Keeper.

1. Stop MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Run /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh script to clear the zookeeper database and logs on zookeeper node. ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs ) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir> 3. Stop zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Start zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 5. Start MoSMB service mosmb start

In case Mo SMB cluster goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing multiple Mo SMB and/or zookeeper nodes to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the whole Mo SMB cluster along with Mo SMB-Zoo Keeper cluster.

1. Stop MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Delete all mo_openfiletable files from pstore path 3. Stop zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Run the zookeeper cleanup script by MoSMB to clear the zookeeper database and logs on all zookeeper nodes in the cluster. <zk-cleanup script> <zookeper-install-dir path> /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.005 onwards) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.003 and before) ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs OR in a workaround copy the clean up script to mosmb-zookeeper node and run it from there.) 5. Start zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 6. Start MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) service mosmb start
Command : EITHER service mosmb status OR systemctl status mosmb Output : MoSMB service is running OR MoSMB service is NOT running
Command : ps aux | grep mosmb OR pidof mosmb Output : root 7521 4.7 4.5 1646804 272104 pts/1 Sl 14:31 0:00 mosmb root 7534 0.0 0.0 112708 976 pts/1 S+ 14:31 0:00 grep --color=auto mosmb

Once you start the Mo SMB service, check the mosmb.log located at default path /var/log/ to see if Mo SMB has started successfully without any errors. For any errors, check the Troubleshooting Guide or FAQs for assistance. Logs directory can be configured as per the requirement by editing parameter logs Path in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json .

In logs directory Mo SMB will create files mosmb.log, mosmb_audit.log, mosmb_vinode.log Please send your queries to support@ryussi.com For mosmb logs, the log level values for all modules can be set in the conf file /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf

FSA_L = 2 SMBCORE_L = 2 CONNECTION_L = 2 MULTICHANNEL_L = 2 IOS_L = 2 RDMA_L = 2 WITNESS_L = 2 OPLOCKS_L = 2 MORPC_L = 2 CONFIG_L = 3 STATISTICS_L = 2 LEASE_L = 2 NDR_L = 2 RPC_L = 2 HASH_L = 2 PST_L = 2 LICENSE_L = 2 AUDIT_L = 2 AUTH_L = 2 ENCRYPTION_L = 2 VINODE_L = 2 DEFAULT_L = 3 COMMON_L = 2 CLUSTER_L = 2 PROFILER_L = 2

The MO_CRITICAL log messages are printed with loglevel set to 0 (They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well) When the log level value set is to 1, error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well) The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 2 in the conf file. (They will be printed for all other log level > 2 as well) For modules CONFIG_L and DEFAULT_L, default value is set to 3, which is MO_INFO.

The INFO logs are printed to give more information. You can choose to disable it and print only error level logs by setting the value to < 3 For module PROFILER_L, default is set to 0. When the value is set to 4, the detailed information is printed. Any changes to log levels can be reloaded at runtime, (no need to restart mosmb) reload Log Level Changes In case debug level is required, following guidelines can be used to set respective log modules to debug level

Login/Access issues- enable AUDIT_L , AUTH_L, SMBCORE_L File I/O issues - enable IOS_L, FSA_L, SMBCORE_L Connection related issues - enable CONNECTION_L , SMBCORE_L Overall general profiling for timings - enable PROFILER_L RPC failures- enable - RPC_L, SMBCORE_L mosmb-zookeeper related issues- VINODE_L, SMBCORE_L
Use these logs for Security Monitoring. One can set the audit module s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3 to get detailed audit logs.

AUDIT_L = 3 This level logs File operations like CREATE RENAME DELETE Or PERMISSION CHANGE With FileName and the User who is attempting the operation Note: Logs for MO_CRIT, MO_ERROR, MO_WARN will also be included
AUDIT_L = 2 This level logs LOGIN and LOGOFF attempts, success or failure, With Time and IP of machine the attempt was made from

The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location (default /var/log). Further details are same as older releases. These are the logs generated by the api used to communicate to the (zookeeper/inode) database. In case of any vinode related errors (either inode Mechanism or zookeeper), user can refer to these logs to get more details. The vinode loglevel starts from 1. For value 0 you may get error and mosmb will not start.
VINODE_L = 1 Or 2/3/4 etc The mosmb_vinode.log log file is populated at the log location, with MO_ERROR,MO_WARN,MO_INFO (Rest could be the Zookeeper logs.)

FSA_L = 0 SMBCORE_L = 0 CONNECTION_L = 0 MULTICHANNEL_L = 0 IOS_L = 0 RDMA_L = 0 WITNESS_L = 0 OPLOCKS_L = 0 MORPC_L = 0 CONFIG_L = 2 STATISTICS_L = 0 LEASE_L = 0 NDR_L = 0 RPC_L = 0 HASH_L = 0 PST_L = 0 LICENSE_L = 0 AUDIT_L = 0 AUTH_L = 0 ENCRYPTION_L = 0 VINODE_L = 0 CLUSTER_L = 0 PROFILER_L = 0 DEFAULT_L = 2
The MO_CRITICAL log messages are printed with loglevel set to -1 (They will be printed for all other log level > -1 as well)

Sample output of critical log in mosmb.log: [2023-02-28 11:16:54.357]:[MO_CRITICAL]:[SMBCORE]:[Thread 4876]: Stopping mosmb as mosmb-zookeeper has got disconnected. Please check mosmb-zookeeper service availability. pid : 4849

When the log level value set is to 0, only error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well) The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 1 in the conf file. (They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well)

Err:-1073741670 [2023-02-28 11:16:49.353]:[MO_WARN]:[SMBCORE]:[Thread 4876]: Default watcher still in progress cannot call zoo_close check_and_reinit_zookeeper_session

Multiple network cards can be added at the server end. In case there are multiple network paths between the client and the server, the SMB3 protocol uses the multi-channel feature. Mo SMB is pre-tuned to perform well for most of the workloads. The Mo SMB performance scales linearly with smb connections. Mo SMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

One can use the performance tuning script provided by Mo SMB at location /etc/mosmb/scripts to apply suggested parameters w.r.t server machines CPU and RAM. To run for smbd parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh smbdirect To run for tcp parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh tcp Detailed usage is provided in script ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh -h

In the Mo SMB configuration, set all the transaction sizes to 8M. (Max Transaction Size, Max Read Size, Max Write Size) Memory allocation is shared in all the TCP connections and Memory utilization is dependent on the following formula: pre Processor Threads * ring Buffer Count * Transaction size Ways to scale-up and scale-out Mo SMB performance: i. In case the client connections are more in number, increase the server threads or pre-processing threads. ii.

The number of threads in the worker thread-pool can be increased so that more number of SMB packets can be served at a time. The number of threads can be increased to 64/128/256 for a high-end system, depending on the CPU cores of the system. iii. The number of pre-allocated buffers can be increased as per the RAM memory. iv. Add an additional network card to make the multi-channel feature functional and enable the multi-channel settings in the Mo SMB configuration. v.

To scale up the Mo SMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. vi. To scale out, add another Mo SMB node to the cluster and expose the same share. Note : Mo SMB provides a performance tuning script as per the server configuration so as Mo SMB will use only upto 60% of the available memory on the server machine.

This script is located at /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh Just run the script to get tuning parameters like pre Processor Threads, thread Pool Size and ring Buffer Count set as per the server configuration.

Note : Refer to the Troubleshooting Guide for the RDMA (SMBDirect) Communication on a Mo SMB Server to understand how to make the RDMA (SMBDirect) basic setup work. The SMBDirect protocol uses the RDMA Read/Write requests to transfer file data within the client and the server. Every RDMA Read/Write operation consists of a SMB2 Read request with 1 or more buffer descriptors . Each buffer descriptor contains information for an I/O buffer with the desired transfer size.

Each buffer descriptor thus requires to be allocated and pinned in memory till the transfer is completed through the RDMA channel. So for every buffer descriptor a buffer of desired size is allocated and pinned, this is a control operation and consumes a significant amount of time. Therefore we have introduced the following parameters to ensure that we reduce control operations during data operations: SMB Direct ring size: i. Default value: 512 ii. Per connection based parameter. iii.

Accepts value in the multiples of 64. iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect when a parameter is changed. The SMB Direct ring size is a parameter that allocates and reserves the specified number of buffers, each of the SMBDirect buffer size size. On every RDMA Read/Write request the buffer is consumed from this ring and replenished after use. This allows faster operations as we can avoid memory pinning operations (control) in the data path resulting in faster speeds.

This can to be tuned w.r.t the I/O pattern for typical application. Setting it to very higher number can guarantee faster transfers at the cost of higher memory footprint. Whereas setting it to very lower number can lead to slower speeds due to the lack of available buffers for transfers. We recommend it to be set 1024 for media applications, and 256 for non-media operations (basic file server, disk io apps) SMB Direct buffer size: i. Default value: 1 ii. Per connection-based parameter. iii.

Accepts value from 1 to 8 inclusive. This value is in Mega Bytes (for example 1 represents 1MB). iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect of a changed parameter. This parameter represents the size of transfer for every buffer descriptor from SMB Direct ring . As per the SMBD Specification this parameter should be less than or equal to the maximum fragmented receive size (1MB). However, this parameter could be less than or equal to the maximum transaction size (8MB) as well.

The SMB client has 1MB value that is the maximum fragmented receive size and hence it the recommended value. TCP parameters i. For RDMA connections only, allocate minimal resources to TCP parameters since the TCP buffers will consume some amount of RAM ii. For example, pre Processor Threads = 4, ring Buffer Count = 256 (or 128 or 64)

This page briefly describes the procedure of creating MS-Distributed File System Namespace (further referred as DFS-N). The DFS-N allows Mo SMB shares on different nodes to be aggregated into a common share list that is visible to SMBClient. The different shares can be hosted from different nodes of Mo SMB servers in cluster. Microsoft Distributed File System(DFS) provides way to aggregate the share list that is hosted on separate Mo SMB servers.

There can more than one entry in the list for supporting a share thereby providing list of servers that can be connected to for that share. This is the way to failover access to share in case of failure of a node. Important : DFS-N provides High Availability but not instant failover. In DFS-N failover, clients attempt to access another target in a referral after one of the targets fails to respond or is no longer part of the namespace.

DFS-N fail over is only performed when a client opens a file or folder. If a client has files or folders open and attempts to read or write to them when the target server is unavailable, the application will receive a failure on that operation. Any I/O that is happening on a Mo SMB node with DFS-N setup will fail if the node goes down and when the application tries to reconnect to the share, the standby node will get connected.

1. Install DFS management on the windows machines - Official reference 2. Creating namespace in Active Directory Server - Open DFS management. Under DFS Management -> Right Click Namespaces -> Create new Namespace. A new wizard will be displayed. Under Namespace -> Right Click created namespace -> New Folder . A new wizard will be displayed. You can create different shares hosted by different Mo SMB server and can see the share map is backed by two SMB nodes

The files which user wants or uses frequently and don t want that files to get truncated, users can pin such files If file is pinned mosmb will make sure that such file will not get truncated during scheduled cache cleanup. To pin the file write permissions are required. While pinning the files if Total Pinned File Size (sum of file sizes of all pinned files) exceeds the 40% of disk space, user will get popup of warning.
While pinning the files if Total Pinned File Size (sum of file sizes of all pinned files) reaches to 50% of disk space, user will get error message and will not be able to pin anymore files.
When file is unpinned it is considered as a normal file and mosmb will allow such files to truncate during cache cleanup. To unpin the file write permissions are required.

All files uploaded directly from s3 storage are considered offline. mosmb provides exclusive download option which allows users to download files. Files downloaded by users are truncated by cache cleanup after a particular duration of time ( threshold parameter in s3.json) or after exceeding cache cleanup trigger limit ( periodicity parameter in s3.json) To download any file read permissions are required.
At any given time only a single file can be pinned, unpinned or downloaded. This functionality is only supported by Windows 10 and above versions. As FILE_ATTRIBUTE_PINNED was introduced in WIN 10.

This feature enables on-demand data downloading. When a user opens a file (e.g., by double-clicking), only the requested portion of the data is retrieved and delivered. This allows seamless video streaming without requiring the entire file to be downloaded in advance. The remaining data continues to download in the background during playback.

a. Truncates the file in local cache which are older than the  threshold  time mentioned in the s3.json. b. Scans the entire local cache in interval of  periodicity  time mentioned in the s3.json and marks the file offline which are truncated from the local cache. c. If "enable Fifo Cleanup" is enabled, it truncates the files in local cache which are older than "threshold" time mentioned in s3.json; when "upper Limit" of cache space is utilized, untill it reaches to "lower Limit".

Two authentication modes of the Mo SMB server provide the POSIX ACL view: POSIX ACL is Portable Operating System Interface - Access Control Lists. Note : POSIX ACL for Mo SMB means only the Server user's ID mapping to the Mo SMB share and its included files and directories.

The ACL view will not display proper names in the Security tabs if: 1. The ldap client configuration is not properly carried out. 2. The File or Directory owner/group is not a domain user. In such cases the names will be displayed as: "LOCAL ACCOUNT" or "S-1-5-113" which is mapped to the owner permissions. If properly configured, the domain user name will be displayed as shown underneath:

In case of posix ACL and ntlm_local authentication you are required to: Set the permissions of the share path to the Linux local user. Add the User group as a Mo SMB user using the mosmb_passwd binary. Refer to ntlm_local Authentication for details. The ownership of the share path should be set to the Linux user and the user group must be set as the Mo SMB user.
In order for Mo SMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.

To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <SPN>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For Mo SMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM (keytab entry of cifs) Command : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, Mo SMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on Mo SMB service start.

Command : sudo klist -A Output : Ticket cache: KCM:0:96571 Default principal: cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting Expires Service principal 05/16/2022 11:33:50 05/16/2022 21:33:50 krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 05/17/2022 11:33:45

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management   Kerberos Policy   Security Settings in the AD. 3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart. 4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at mosmb start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

sssd helps during the User ID mapping for domain users. If in case sssd is not installed before, install sssd-common and configure sssd on all the Mo SMB server machine in cluster. Create a sssd configuration file for Mo SMB /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf and it should contain parameters as shown below.

[sssd] domains = mosmbad.dom config_file_version = 2 services = nss, pam [domain/mosmbad.dom] ad_domain = mosmbad.dom krb5_realm = MOSMBAD.DOM krb5_keytab = /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab realmd_tags = manages-system joined-with-adcli cache_credentials = True id_provider = ad krb5_store_password_if_offline = True default_shell = /bin/bash ldap_id_mapping = True use_fully_qualified_names = True fallback_homedir = /home/%u@%d access_provider = ad
sssd configuration files must be at /etc/sssd/ with permissions of file set to 600 (-rw-------) and for both /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf
Verify that nsswitch.conf file is configured to use SSSD as a provider Open /etc/nsswitch.conf file and verify that it points to sss for the name service switch
Restart the sssd service after making any changes in the sssd configurations.
In the mosmb.json file set following for S3 share :

For each object in the bucket on the object store, a local representation of the object thumbnail is created in cache on the local share by the Mo SMB S3 client. For these thumbnails, owner and group permissions are set to "root" by the Mo SMB S3 client. Here particular user or group access is supported through inherited permissions of the parent directory.

s3-share is share name with share path /mnt/data/s3-share The share path permissions set are as follows (permissions are set using setfacl command): $ getfacl /mnt/data/s3-share getfacl: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names # file: mnt/data/s3-share # owner: foo@abc.dom # group: bar@abc.dom user::rwx group::rwx mask::rwx other::r-x default:user::rwx default:user:acluser@abc.dom:rwx default:group::rwx default:group:aclgroup@abc.dom:r-x default:mask::rwx default:other::r-x

Here, the first four lines describe access permissions given to user, group and other which is rwx, rwx and r-x respectively. The mask:rwx acts as permissions filter (or it indicates maximum) for other user or group present in permissions list.

If permissions for mask are set as mask:rwx this means there is no filtering of permissions for any other user or group present in permissions If permissions for mask are set as mask:r-x means that any user or group even if it has say rwx permissions it will effectively get only r-x permissions. The next lines in the permissions list start with the default keyword are the permissions which will transfer to the child file or directory created under this directory (inherited permissions).

If thumbnail name say  pictures  and present under s3-share directory i.e. /mnt/data/s3-share/pictures then it will have permissions as: $ getfacl /mnt/data/s3-share/pictures/ getfacl: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names # file: mnt/data/s3-share/Pictures/ # owner: root # group: root user::rwx user:acluser@abc.dom:rwx group::rwx group:aclgroup@abc.dom:r-x mask::rwx other::r-x default:user::rwx default:user:acluser@abc.dom:rwx default:group::rwx default:group:aclgroup@abc.dom:r-x default:mask::rwx default:other::r-x
Here as mentioned earlier the client created thumbnail directory pictures has user and group set to root user. It will have acluser and aclgroup with access permissions rwx and r-x respectively.

As mentioned in earlier section (working) the object thumbnails owner and group permissions are set to  root . So only root user can add/modify/delete the permissions setting for that file or directory. Considering earlier example if one wants to have rwx permissions for aclgroup then those changes can be done only by root user.
To know more about how windows side permissions are mapped on linux server/clients please go through " Advance_Posix_ACL#Permission_mapping_Windows_to_Linux "

Checkmk is an open-source IT monitoring platform that helps monitor IT infrastructure, including Servers, Networks. It can be configured to communicate with Mo SMB nodes to monitor Mo SMB logs and smb statistics
Download the required version from the link below. Steps for installation of the server are available online on Checkmk site and also given below for quick reference.

Update your OS packages with apt update OR yum update commands depending on your OS version Once the package is downloaded, install the package, if prompted for resolving dependencies press  Y  to proceed

For creating a monitoring site on Checkmk server, below command can be executed. In this example local site named server_monitor will be created & login details will be shared once the process completes.
Commands that can be executed to display the status of the site created and to start. By default it will be in a stopped state

Once the site has started, it can be accessed by using the IP or the hostname of the Checkmk Server node, using a web-browser followed by the username & password provided earlier during site creation Once you log in, the default password for the site can be changed from the main menu left panel   User   Change password

The Checkmk agent consists of the agent script and the Agent Controller, which communicates with the Agent Receiver on the Checkmk server Before the agent can be installed on all nodes to be monitored, some rules are required to be added on Checkmk server.

# Go to "Setup" -> "Agents"-> "Windows, Linux, Solaris, AIX" # Click on the "Agent rules" # Select the "Text logfiles (Linux, Solaris, Windows)" # Add below logfile paths under the "Configure a logfile section" /var/log/mosmb.log /var/log/mosmb_audit.log /var/log/mosmb_vinode.log
State -> Critical, Pattern(Regex) -> \[MO_CRITICAL\].* State -> Critical, Pattern(Regex) -> \[MO_ERROR\].* State -> WARNING, Pattern(Regex) -> \[MO_WARN\].*

Save it by clicking on the "Save" button on the top Click on the fire icon, "Bake agents ; this will create a Checkmk Agent package with all of the settings added Once the Checkmk Agent is Baked, the required Checkmk agent package can be downloaded in any of the formats RPM,DEB etc.

Choose depending on your OS on Mo SMB nodes Once the Checkmk agent is downloaded, the file can be copied to every node using scp or any other method Here in this example, command used to install the package on redhat based Mo SMB nodes

Once installation of the Checkmk agent is done on all required Mo SMB nodes, they need to be added to the Checkmk server for monitoring For adding Mo SMB nodes to Checkmk, navigate through the main menu on left panel Setups -> Hosts ->Add host and fill out the required host details like Host Name, IP etc.

Then click on Save & run service discovery Once Save & run service discovery is clicked, it will take a few seconds to complete the scan and show all the services it scanned on all Mo SMB nodes & display a screen in which we can select Accept all or we can choose which all services need to be monitored by Checkmk server (click the green + symbol to add the services one by one to Checkmk After selecting required services, the exclamation mark on the right side top corner will display the number of changes that need to be done.

Clicking the exclamation mark will redirect the user to an   Activation status   tab shown in the image below. The user needs to click on the red activation button that starts activating the services Then register the Checkmk agents on all the nodes of the Mo SMB cluster. The Checkmk agent registration can be done using the Checkmk agent controller  cmk-agent-ctl  supported with the systemd version 219 or newer.

# To get the systemd version systemctl --version # To get the Checkmk agent controller version cmk-agent-ctl --version # To register the Checkmk agent cmk-agent-ctl register  hostname <HOSTNAME>  server <SERVER NAME/IP>::8000  site <SITE NAME>  user <USER NAME>  password <PASSWORD> # To verify the trust relationship after successful registration cmk-agent-ctl status

Once the Mo SMB nodes are registered as per the above step then the user needs to go to the Checkmk server & navigate through Monitor -> Overview -> All Hosts , which will show all the nodes that are being monitored. Clicking one of the nodes will display all the services which are being monitored on that particular node. The page refreshes every 30 seconds. It might take a few minutes to show all the changes. Every service we can click it and brows through for additional information

Mo SMB server Now supports NTLMSSP authentication from Release versions:3.1.1_004 and above. 6. Configure required authorization (ACL) settings 7.

Setup the Mo SMB configuration file (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json) for standalone use. In global Section set enable High Availability to no In section node Info Section , give any integer between 1 to 1023 to node Id

"globalSection": { .... "enableHighAvailability" : "no" .... "authSection": { "authMode": "krb5" } "nodeInfoSection": { "nodeId": 1 } "vinodeSection" : { "mechanism" : "inode", .... } "sharesSection": [ { "shareName": "Local Share", "comment": "File Share Directory", "sharePath": "/opt/MoSMB_share", "isContinouslyAvailable": "yes", "isCluster": "no", ....
8. Set Priority List for AD IPs (in a AD Farm) if required. If you have a AD farm, and want mosmb to use only few of them for any reason:
Add Priority List for AD IPs in /etc/mosmb

9. If the server machines are centos(or similar) 8 and above supported encryption options needs to be enabled from server side as well in order for Kerberos authentication to work completely. On every Cent OS 8.3 and above Mo SMB server, where authentication against AD Domain Controllers takes place, use the update-crypto-policies command to enable the AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy in addition to the DEFAULT cryptographic policy.

Command : update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Output : Setting system policy to DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Command : init 6 Notes : 1. System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up so it is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies to fully take place. 3. The AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy is only available on CentOS 8.3 and newer.

To enable support for RC4 in Cent OS 8.2 , create and enable a custom cryptographic module policy with cipher = RC4-128+. For more details, see Customizing system-wide cryptographic policies with policy modifiers. Reference links :
To enable support for RC4 in Cent OS 8.0 and Cent OS 8.1 , add +rc4 to the permitted_enctypes option in the /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/krb5.config file
[libdefaults] permitted_enctypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 camellia256-cts-cmac aes128-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes128-cts-hmac-sha256-128 camellia128-cts-cmac +rc4

NTACL is the Windows authorization mechanism; therefore it is supported only with Kerberos authentication. In order to enable NTACL support for a Mo SMB share which is newly created path or any old path which does not have any extended attributes, follow bellow steps: In share Section under acl Strategy set parameter acl to ntacl In share Section set parameter metadata Store to xattr

"authSection": { "authMode": "krb5" } "sharesSection": [ { "shareName": "Local Share", "comment": "File Share Directory", "sharePath": "/opt/MoSMB_share", "aclStrategy":{ "acl": "ntacl", .... } "metadataStore": "xattr" ....

2. Setting / Adding ACEs and Owner from Windows client Follow these steps once complete Kerberos setup is carried out and share is accessible with FQDN of server/cluster. 1. Access server from client and right click on the share for which you want to set the permissions and Open share Properties   Security 2. Initially there are no owner set to the share. To check owner click on Advanced settings. 3. To add an owner for the share, click on "Owner" : "Change" 4.

To add ACEs , click on "Edit" and select "Add" to add your user to the share from share properties window. 5. Set User permissions for the added User and Click on "Apply" . 7. Follow the same steps to add more users/groups. Note: Rename and delete operations with NTACLs work when modify permission is given to the file/directory while setting permissions. 3.

Inheritance in ACEs If the share has existing files/folders inside it before acl settings are configured or change the configuration to "ntacl" please check the following settings: 1. Right click on share. Open Properties   Security   Advanced   Add 2. Click on Select a principal and add a user to the share as specified in step 2 .5 3. Ensure that the  Applies to  option is set to  this folder, subfolders and files . 4.

Initially, if the Owner is not set for the share, the logged in user of the client is automatically set as the owner of the share as well as folders and sub folders. 5. Inheritance: Enable inheritance while assigning permissions to the share or a folder by clicking on Properties   Security   Advanced   Enable Inheritance . Note: Time taken to apply inherited permissions will be dependent on the number of files/folders in the share.

1. Initially no acls are applied to the share contents and the user cannot see the owner. The share is not accessible to any user/group until ACEs are applied to the share. 2. Rename and delete operations with NTACLs work when modify permission is given to the file/directory while setting permissions. 3. Currently  This folder, subfolder and files  option is supported only for  Applied to:  field. 4. Time taken to apply inherited permissions will be dependent on the number of files/folders in the share.

Mo SMB provides the zookeeper server cleanup script for local zookeeper nodes. Notes : 1. Script should run when Mo SMB server is not running. 2. This script can be used only for the local zookeeper server. 3. When zookeeper is not running, this script can not remove the nodes.
[Older Releases] -- /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh [Newer Releases] -- /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh

This script is used to cleanup the zookeeper server node/s. It will remove all the nodes except for the /mosmb_s3_root and zookeeper s own nodes. When and if Mo SMB nodes inside zookeeper are directed under special  chrootsuffix  then only the nodes under configured path are removed except for the /mosmb_s3_root and zookeeper s own nodes.

Command:  zk-cleanup.sh -h OR mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh -h zk-cleanup.sh <zookeeper_dir_path> OR mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeeper_dir_path> Here, zookeeper_dir_path is the path where MoSMB-Zookeeper in installed.
Output:  dataDir in /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg is dataDir=/var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb .... Cleaned zookeeper files

1. One EDF node should have the clustershell configured for executing commands in parallel on a cluster. 2. Identify the nodes on which Mo SMB-Zoo Keeper would be installed (add the group to clustershell (clush) configuration so that it can be used for Mo SMB-Zoo Keeper installation configuration and management.) 3. Install the mosmb-zookeeper rpm on all zk nodes
data Dir =<update the dedicated zookeeper data folder> Update all the zookeeper node entries under Server List in the zoo.cfg
Create data directory if not present on all mosmb-zookeeper nodes Create myid files in the data directory of each mosmb-zookeeper node with same id as mentioned in zoo.cfg server list

This step is not mandatory. In case zookeeper restarts, the server side log located in the log folder of zookeeper installation path, (/opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/) is overwritten and old logs are over-written/lost. To avoid loss of old logs, one can do the following 1. A solution is to modify zk Env.sh and set ZOO_LOG4J_PROP
$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ] ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE"
$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ] ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE, ROLLINGFILE "
3. Now every restart of mosmb-zookeeper service will have two log files, one current (.out) and one backed up with old logs (.log).

The Mo SMB server authenticates the AD Domain users using Kerberos authentication method. This requires a Kerberos ticket granted to the server by configured AD machine. This ticket generation can be done using the kinit commands.
Command 1 : yum search kerberos Output 1 : .... krb5-workstation.x86_64 : Kerberos 5 programs for use on workstations Command 2 : yum install krb5-workstation.x86_64
Command 1 : apt-cache search krb5 Output 1 : .... krb5-user - basic programs to authenticate using MIT Kerberos Command 2 : apt-get install krb5-user
Make sure these entries are present in the /etc/krb5.conf file. Modify libdefaults section - rdns, default_ccache_name entries are present refer - cache type limitations
includedir /etc/krb5.conf.d/ [libdefaults] .......... rdns = false default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt ..........
[realms] MOSMBAD.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom master_kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom } [domain_realm] mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM .mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM

# cat /etc/krb5.conf | grep default_ccache_name default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/mosmb_kerberos_ticket OR default_ccache_name = DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_ticket_dir #default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid} [not supported] # cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/kcm_default_ccache | grep default_ccache_name default_ccache_name = KCM:
Mo SMB will support only cache type FILE for kerberos versions below 1.13 The FILE and DIR path can be of your choice. Make sure path is anything other than /tmp for persistence

Assumption is domain join is carried out successfully and there are no restricted entries for kerberos on configured AD. If restricted entries are present on AD side please go through Removing restricted Kerberos entries Run the ktutil to Read and add existing host keys to /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab from krb5.keytab and then add required entries.

ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q

ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM: ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM: ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q

The domain user used in above commands and the user used to generate the ticket (as explained in next few steps using kinit) should be the same. The domain name in above commands must be in uppercase. If and when the user password or user spn is changed Kerberos keytab related settings are needed to carry out again

When there are restricted Kerberos entries on the server machine seen as output of command klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab Check if they are also present on the AD side if present we need to remove them from AD, Login to AD machine with user who has admin rights and open Active directory administrative Center In computers open properties of the Mo SMB server machine In Properties of server machine   Extensions   Attribute Editor In Attribute editor select the attribute "service Principal Name" Remove restricted entries from the service Principal Name against the Mo SMB server.
After making changes on AD machine destroy the previously taken Kerberos tickets if any from server machine with kdestroy -A and generate new ticket.
To generate a ticket on the server use the kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> command. The command asks password for the specified user.
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command : sudo kinit -V mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Output : Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal, and -t will refer to the keytab file specified
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM

The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials. Also check the ticket time with klist -a, (time of Mo SMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries will be in the format of user@DOMAIN / cifs/user.domain@DOMAIN for standalone server, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive . To generate a ticket on the server, Mo SMB will use principal name from domain_list.txt, match against the one present in mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket.

# klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab name: FILE:/etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab KVNO Principal---- -------------------------------------------- 3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
